keyRules: Maximum 120 m above the ground (EASA Open Category)
Registration as a UAS operator (15 € / 3 years), if the drone weighs 250 g or more or has a camera
A1/A3 online training is required – minimum age 16
Night flight is permitted under the EASA open-category baseline when the required green flashing light is used, unless a current geographic zone or national rule imposes a stricter condition.
Minimum distance of 1 km from residential areas (applies mainly to Category A3—stricter requirements for heavier drones)
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) with the drone
Do not fly over assemblies of people. Rules for uninvolved persons depend on subcategory, class mark and operating conditions; check the official matrix.
Northern Cyprus (TRNC): separate jurisdiction – different rules
UN No-Fly Zone: Total Flight Ban
importantNotes: Night flight is permitted under the EASA open-category baseline when the required green flashing light is used, unless a current geographic zone or national rule imposes a stricter condition.
A 1-kilometer distance from residential areas is very restrictive on a small island
The 8-kilometer safety zones around airports cover a large part of the island
Northern Cyprus (TRNC) is a separate jurisdiction with different rules
The UN Dead Zone is a strict no-fly zone

fields.disclaimerText: Before every flight, always check the official sources of the DCA Cyprus and drones.gov.cy. This app is a tool—not a legal authority. Cyprus follows EASA regulations with strict national additions, such as a ban on night flights.
